prs.title: Align README with NST Capstone 2 repository standard.
prs.body: Restructure project documentation to the official format while preserving the diabetes readmission context, KPI framework, submission checklist, and contribution matrix placeholders.

prs.title: Improved email API reliability with error handling and safe response.
prs.body:
This update improves the resilience of the email API by adding structured error handling.
Right now, a failed request throws an unhandled exception, which can break UI flows. With this change:

- Errors are safely caught and returned in a consistent format
- Debugging becomes easier with clearer error logs
- No behavioural or interface changes, fully backward compatible

It simply makes the API more reliable to consume in real-world UI usage.

Happy to refine further if needed...

prs.title: Added Swiper carousel in HeroSection with larger banner images for better User Interface.
refactor: removed jQuery carousel and added React-Swiper implementation for smooth, responsive slider.
Optimised normalize-internal-links.js for clarity and performance.
prs.body:
### What does this PR do?
This PR optimizes normalize-internal-links.js for better readability and performance.
It keeps the existing behavior fully intact while:

• Removing noisy development-only console logs  
• Reducing unnecessary DOM scans and MutationObserver triggers  
• Streamlining the link-normalization logic for easier long-term maintenance  

Overall, the script becomes cleaner, faster, and more predictable without changing any functionality.


### How did you verify your code works?
I verified the update by checking internal link behaviour across multiple docs pages, ensuring target and rel attributes were still removed correctly. I also triggered a few DOM changes to confirm the MutationObserver kept normalizing new links as expected. Finally, I tested both dev and production modes to make sure logging behaved correctly and the functionality remained unchanged. Everything continues to work as before, just more efficiently.

prs.title: Improve error handling with Sentry integration.
prs.body: This update refines the existing ErrorBoundary by improving its stability and clarity without altering its core behavior. The cleanup removes development-only console logs and ensures the captured error context is consistent for Sentry. Overall, the component becomes quieter, cleaner, and more aligned with production best practices while continuing to fall back gracefully to the 404 view.

prs.title: Update browser.md with Mongoose limitations
prs.body:
The current browser.md explains that the browser build is limited, but it doesn’t clearly list what actually works. This update adds a short note to make the behavior more understandable.

The note now clarifies that:
• Browser build supports only schema-based validation. • Built-in validators and custom validators work with validate()/validateSync(). • Setters/getters work as expected.
• Models, queries, defaults, middleware, and all database operations are not
  available in the browser build.

This makes the browser section clearer and reduces confusion for developers using validation on the frontend.

Refactor backpack styles with new design elements
prs.body:
-Retro gaming font: 'Press Start 2P' which gives pixel-art RPG vibes. 
-Vibrant gradients & glow effects which are more alive and modern. 
-Smooth hover animations - subtle scale, glow, and lift. 
-Glass-like transparency inside frames and containers. 
-Dashed borders for empty slots which is visually clear they’re empty. 
-Consistent shadows to mimic depth and light.

prs.title: Update banner.js
prs.body:
-Idempotent Banner Injection: Checks if the file already has a banner (/*! or /**) to prevent duplicates.

-Async / Non-blocking I/O: Uses fs.promises for modern, asynchronous file operations.

-Atomic Write: Writes to a temporary file first, then renames it to avoid corruption or partial writes.

-Preserves File Metadata: Attempts to retain original file permissions and timestamps.

-Robust Path Handling: Supports optional file argument and resolves package.json reliably.

-Better Error Handling: Exits with a non-zero code if an error occurs, making it CI-friendly.

-Future-proof / Maintainable: Clean, readable, and structured for easy updates or integration into npm scripts or CI pipelines.

prs.title: Update 404.html - Making it more Professional and Impressive
prs.body:
-Background and Font Colors: Using a neutral background (#f8f9fa) for the body and a soft gray for the text color.
-Centered Page Layout: flex properties are used on the body to ensure the error message is centered vertically and horizontally.
-Card-Like Container: A white, rounded container with a subtle shadow improves readability and makes the error message stand out.
-Error Code Style: The 404 message in red is bold and catches the user’s attention.
-"Back to Home" Button: A button is added with hover effects, encouraging users to return to the home page if they get lost.

prs.title: Update index.html and making it more professional
prs.body:
-Background Color: Changed to a light grey for a clean look.
Heading Colors: Updated colors to be more modern and cohesive.
-List Items: Added hover effects and shadows for a more interactive feel.
-Buttons: Rounded with a hover effect for better interaction.
-Media Queries: Added for better responsiveness on smaller screens.

prs.title: Update registration.css
prs.body:
- Enhanced CSS for a modern, professional look:
  - Applied a gradient background to the body for a more engaging appearance.
  - Increased padding and adjusted container width for better spacing.
  - Updated button styles with hover effects for improved interactivity.
  - Enhanced input fields with focus effects to guide user interaction.
  - Redesigned social icons with hover effects for better accessibility.
  - Ensured responsive design for mobile devices.

- Minor HTML adjustments for improved accessibility and structure.

These changes aim to enhance user experience and align with modern web design standards."

prs.title: Update fast-api-main.py
prs.body:
-Cleaner Comments: Added clear, straightforward comments throughout the code to make it easier to understand at a glance.
-Enhanced Browser Function: The function that opens the browser now has a simple description explaining what it does, making it more user-friendly.
-Improved Naming Consistency: Updated the naming of project settings so everything matches up nicely, which should make the code feel more cohesive.
-Organized Imports: Rearranged the imports to be in a more logical order, which just makes the code look cleaner and more professional overall.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body:
Enhanced the repository README for clarity and professionalism by centering key elements, refining language for better readability, and organizing the contribution steps with clear structure. Replaced long paragraphs with concise descriptions, added specific guidelines for file naming and directory structure, and emphasized HTML/CSS-only contributions.

Thank you for making a pull request to ZTM's Animation Nation project. We will review your pull request as soon as we can, but keep in mind we can get very busy during Hacktoberfest.

While you're waiting, please look over this checklist. Feel free to write a description of what you did as well!

## What I did

*Optional: Edit this to tell us what you did!*

## Pull request checklist

- [x] This pull request uses **HTML and CSS** only and provides a `meta.json` file.
- [x] All files in this pull request are in a directory named: `<github_username>-<art_name>` without the <>.
- [x] Your pull request contains `index.html` and `styles.css`.
	- [x] Your html file is named exactly: `index.html`
	- [x] Your css file is named exactly: `styles.css`
- [x] Your pull request your `meta.json` with:
	- [x] `githubHandle`: being your unique github user name,
	- [x] `artName` : name of your animation
- [ ] Your submission includes **at least one animation**.
- [x] Your pull request **does not modify any other files** in the repository.

#### Not normally needed
- [x] Check this if you would like to discuss an improvement besides adding animations

**Note: if your pull request makes an improvement besides adding a CSS animation you will need to discuss this with the maintainers. You can help kickstart this process by explaining what your change does and why you are making it in the "What I did" section**

## What to expect

- If your pull request meets the checklist requirements it will be approved and merged by a maintainer
- If your pull request needs some changes. A maintainer will leave a comment requesting changes
- If your pull request significantly violates the criteria in the checklist it will be closed with feedback.
